step_1_context.text: Some verbs can be followed either by an object + -ing or a possessive + -ing with a similar meaning, although the possessive + -ing form is usually considered to be rather formal:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule:
Some verbs can be followed either by an object + -ing or a possessive + -ing with a similar meaning, although the possessive + -ing form is usually considered to be rather formal:
 • I resented Tom winning the prize.
step_2_explanation.formula:
Some verbs can be followed by to + -ing where to is a preposition:
 • She confessed to stealing the money.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake:
Other verbs can be followed by different prepositions + -ing:
 • by + -ing: Can you begin by cleaning the floors.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 30.1.1 I really hate you having to be away from home so much. (Rewrite using possessive)
options: I really hate your having to be away...
I really hate you to be away...
I really hate you being away...
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: I really hate your having to be away...
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Possessive form of the object before -ing.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 30.1.3 I have always detested the dog jumping up at me. (Rewrite using possessive)
options: I have always detested the dog's jumping up at me.
I have always detested the dog jumping at me.
I have always detested how the dog jumps.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: I have always detested the dog's jumping up at me.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Possessive form is possible with 'the dog'.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 30.2.1 In a survey, 65% of dentists said that they had made mistakes... (Rewrite with 'owned up')
30.3.1 I heard the tyre ___ and then the lorry skidded across the road.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Both are possible, but 'burst' might suggest the completed sound of the pop.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 30.3.3 She felt the wasp ___ her just before she brushed it off her arm.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Both -ing and bare infinitive are possible with 'feel'.
